business.headline: Petrol, diesel, CNG prices hiked again. Check rates in your cityIndia relies on imports for nearly 85 per cent of its crude oil requirements, making domestic fuel prices vulnerable to fluctuations in global markets. The fresh hike is likely to put additional pressure on commuters, transporters and businesses across multiple sectors.

India better positioned on inflation, RBI should keep rates unchanged: ASSOCHAMASSOCHAM's analysis showed that India's retail inflation increased only slightly from 3.2% in February 2026 to 3.5% in April 2026 — a rise of just 0.3 percentage points. In comparison, inflation in the United States jumped sharply from 2.4% to 3.8% during the same period.

RBI to transfer record Rs 2.87 lakh crore dividend to govt for FY26This move is expected to give the government additional financial room at a time of geopolitical uncertainty and global economic challenges.

Don't file your ITR blindly: These 6 AIS mismatches can invite an income tax noticeThink filing your ITR is just about checking pre-filled details and clicking 'submit'? Not so fast. As the ITR filing season for FY 2026–27 begins, tax experts say ignoring even a small mismatch in your AIS could land you in trouble later.

Haryana is a home away from home for Japanese firms: CM Nayab Singh SainiSpeaking at the third India-Japan Conclave in New Delhi, organised by India Today, Saini said Haryana has become a "home away from home" for Japanese companies and stressed that the relationship between the two sides is built not only on investment, but also on trust.

8th Pay Commission: Why a full return to old pension scheme may be difficult nowAs the 8th Pay Commission widens consultations, employee unions are renewing their push for the Old Pension Scheme. But with Rs 16.5 lakh crore already inside NPS, the debate is shifting towards guaranteed pension safeguards within the existing system.
Sensex, Nifty end week higher as banking shares drive gainsThe BSE Sensex ended 231.99 points, or 0.31%, higher at 75,415.35. The Nifty 50 gained 64.60 points, or 0.27%, to close at 23,719.30.

Why your next pack of condoms may cost more amid global oil shockThe company, which sells Manforce condoms and holds around 30% market share in the country, said it may eventually pass on higher costs to customers if the situation in the Middle East worsens.

Japan bets big on India with $68 billion investment target, tech pushAt the third India-Japan Conclave organised by the India Today Group, Japan unveiled an updated Indo-Pacific strategy that places India at the heart of its push on technology, investment and regional security.
Sensex up 500 points: Why is stock market rising today?At 1:45 PM, the BSE Sensex was up 514.96 points, or 0.68%, at 75,698.32. The index touched an intraday high of 75,810.97 and a low of 75,230.75.

Is India sleepwalking into a retirement crisis?Many Indians dream of a peaceful retirement, but dreams alone do not pay medical bills or monthly expenses. So, are Indians financially ready for retirement, or just hoping for the best?

From long queues to quick clicks: How innovation is reshaping digital lendingApplying for a loan is no longer just about filling forms and waiting for approvals. From instant verification to quicker disbursals, innovation is reshaping digital lending, but what does this shift really mean for borrowers?

RBI may skip repo rate hike despite rupee fall, eye inflation: ReportThe RBI is expected to focus more on inflation and broader economic stability even as the rupee continues to hit record lows against the US dollar amid the ongoing global oil shock.

Soft landing, not free fall: Decoding RBI's rupee game planThe central bank is increasingly allowing the rupee to weaken gradually as oil prices remain high, with economists arguing that preventing panic matters more than defending a symbolic number.

8th Pay Commission consultation moves to Lucknow: Key dates, eligibility and detailsThe 8th Pay Commission is steadily expanding its consultation process, with Lucknow now added to the list of cities where employee representatives and government bodies can share their views. But who can meet the panel, and what are the key dates you should know?

Rupee at 100 against US dollar? Why experts say India shouldn't panicAs crude oil prices stay elevated and the rupee weakens further, a growing number of experts now argue India's real risk is not crossing 100 against the dollar, but rising inflation and slowing growth.

Parle Industries hits 5% upper circuit again as investors chase wrong Parle stockParle Industries shares hit the upper circuit for a third straight session after the Modi-Meloni Melody video went viral. The rally reflected confusion over the Parle name, even though Melody toffees are made by unlisted Parle Products.

Sensex, Nifty open higher as crude stays near $105; banks, financials lead gainsThe BSE Sensex was up 336.46 points at 75,519.82 around 9:26 am, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 86.30 points to trade at 23,741.00.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will markets rise on easing crude prices or stay volatile?Dalal Street is set for a near-flat start as crude hovers near $105 and traders watch US-Iran talks. Foreign selling, mixed technical signals and domestic buying are keeping the market range-bound.

Not Saudi, not US: India's newest major oil supplier may surprise youVenezuela became India's third-largest crude supplier in May, overtaking Saudi Arabia and the United States. The shift shows how lower prices and regional supply disruptions are reshaping India's crude sourcing.

entertainment.headline: Pan-India before pan-India: Malayalam cinema comes full circle with Drishyam 3 Mohanlal and Jeethu Joseph's Drishyam 3 brings renewed focus to Malayalam cinema's long national reach. Its arrival underlines how rooted, emotionally precise stories travelled across India long before pan-India became a formula.

Cannes 2026 gets a bestie moment with Eva Longoria and Aishwarya Rai. Watch Eva Longoria and Aishwarya Rai Bachchan shared a warm exchange during a Cannes photo opportunity. A video from the beautiful moment showed Eva referring to Aishwarya as her "best friend" at the festival.

Drishyam 3 box office Day 2: Mohanlal's film wins overseas, Rs 75 crore worldwide Mohanlal's Drishyam 3 is rewriting box office records overseas, earning a staggering Rs 75.94 crore worldwide in just two days. With Georgekutty pulling crowds globally, the thriller is now racing towards the Rs 100-crore mark.

Still soaking in love: Karishma Tanna gives insight into her baby shower ceremony Actor Karishma Tanna shared a video from her South Indian-style baby shower ceremony. She and husband Varun Bangera are gearing up to welcome their first child.

Aishwarya Rai's Cannes 2026 look drips in blue drama and main-character energy Aishwarya Rai Bachchan, India's Cannes veteran, made her first appearance at the ongoing film festival in a structured blue gown. A video of her look went viral on social media on Friday night.

Madhuri Dixit, Triptii Dimri trolled as co-star Dharna gets snubbed by paps At a Mumbai promotion for Maa Behen, paparazzi asked for a frame with only Madhuri Dixit and Triptii Dimri. Several social media users have now questioned the silence around the exclusion of influencer-actor Dharna Durga from the photo-call.

Rukmini Vasanth's viral bikini shoot clips spark online buzz Visuals appearing to show Rukmini Vasanth during a poolside shoot have surfaced online. The clips have drawn wide attention, though the purpose of the shoot remains unconfirmed.

Prosecutors accuse Matthew Perry's assistant of playing key role in overdose case New court filings reveal how Matthew Perry's assistant allegedly became deeply involved in the actor's ketamine use before his fatal overdose. Here's what prosecutors and Perry's family have said.

Ranbir interviews Nitesh Tiwari, talks Ramayana's 'intimidating' world-building Filmmaker Nitesh Tiwari has opened up about feeling intimidated while taking on Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana. Here's what the director said about the pressure, responsibility and scale of the ambitious film.

Dhurandhar: Raw & Undekha on Netflix adds more cuss words, not new scenes Netflix has begun streaming Dhurandhar: Raw & Undekha as an alternate cut of the film. However, the version keeps previously muted cuss words and adds no new scenes or violence.

Maa Behen trailer: Chaos, comedy and a dead body in Madhuri Dixit's film The trailer for Maa Behen has dropped, teasing crime, family drama and complete chaos as Madhuri Dixit and Triptii Dimri get caught up in a bizarre mystery.

Patriot's Zarin Shihab calls Mammootty an incredibly generous co-actor Zarin Shihab spoke about acting in Malayalam hit Patriot alongside Mammootty and Mohanlal. She also discussed preparing for the role of Dr Ayesha Iqbal.

The internet loses it over fake Khloe Kardashian and Michael B Jordan photos Viral photos showing Khloe Kardashian and Michael B Jordan together sent social media into a frenzy, but online users soon spotted something unusual about the images.

Kamal Haasan shares 4-minute video backing PM Modi's energy-saving appeal Actor-politician Kamal Haasan backed Prime Minister Narendra Modi's call to save energy amid rising global tensions. He shared a four-minute video online to emphasise that the impact of the conflict involving the US, Israel and Iran would be felt in India.

Arya Babbar reacts to old affair rumours about father Raj Babbar and Rekha Arya Babbar responded with humour when asked about old rumours linking Raj Babbar and Rekha. He said any attraction would be human and cautioned against overreading celebrity gossip.

Rs 450 crore for Ramayana? Makers aim for Bollywood's biggest theatrical deal yet Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana is in talks for a record Rs 450 crore Hindi theatrical deal. The negotiations have sharpened industry focus on the film's scale, valuation and wider rights strategy.

Won't marry secretly: Kangana sets record straight on married look in viral video Kangana Ranaut said the viral images of her in a married look were from a shoot. She denied secret wedding rumours and said she would not marry in secret.

Sam Thompson screams, jumps on Tom Hiddleston in viral fanboy moment. Watch Sam Thompson screamed with excitement and jumped into a bear hug with Tom Hiddleston at a London event. The clip quickly went viral, with fans saying his reaction matched their own.

general news.headline: Petrol, diesel, CNG prices hiked again. Check rates in your cityIndia relies on imports for nearly 85 per cent of its crude oil requirements, making domestic fuel prices vulnerable to fluctuations in global markets. The fresh hike is likely to put additional pressure on commuters, transporters and businesses across multiple sectors.

Now, Cockroach Janta Party's founder claims his Insta account hackedThe development comes just hours after CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke publicly stated that his personal Instagram account had allegedly been hacked and that he had lost access to it.

Trump's man Marco Rubio arrives in Kolkata, to meet PM Modi in Delhi later today  Live UpdatesUS Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in Kolkata on Saturday for his first India visit, ahead of talks with PM Narendra Modi in Delhi on trade, defence, energy and Quad cooperation. Rubio's four-day tour includes Kolkata, Agra, Jaipur and New Delhi, and comes ahead of a key Quad meeting on May 26.

Virat Kohli ignores handshake with Travis Head, moment goes viral: WatchVirat Kohli sparked controversy as he ignored the customary post-match handshake with Travis Head following the SRH vs RCB game in Hyderabad.

Why Himanta govt named a Guwahati flyover after a Bengali nationalist leaderAssam's Chief Minister, Himanta Biswa Sarma, has named Guwahati's new Rs 376 crore flyover after Syama Prasad Mookerjee, the founder of the Bharatiya Jana Sangh (BJS). It's not just because the BJS was the BJP's precursor. There's a reason in the history you might want to know.

Delhi gets breather from severe heat, dust storm, showers to bring down mercuryThe RWFC has warned of dust storms, thunderstorms, light rain and lightning across parts of Delhi-NCR and nearby states over the next few hours. Officials have advised caution as strong winds may reduce visibility, disrupt traffic and create localised outages.

31 of the 50 hottest cities in the world today are in UP. Here's whyAQI.in rankings on Friday evening showed that all 50 of the world's hottest cities were in India, with more than half of them located in Uttar Pradesh.

Army says men in uniform at AAP-RJD presser are ex-servicemen dismissed earlierThe Indian Army said four former servicemen seen at a press conference with AAP MP Sanjay Singh and RJD MP Manoj Jha were either dismissed from service or declared deserters. It accused them of spreading misleading claims online and urged citizens to remain alert to misinformation.

A cape, satin couture, crystal florals define Aishwarya Rai Bachchan's second Cannes outingAishwarya Rai Bachchan attended Lights on Women's Worth at Cannes in a blush pink Sophie Couture gown with Aaradhya Bachchan by her side. The appearance marked a shift towards a more sculpted festival silhouette in her 24th year at the event.
The Cockroach RepublicSatirist Kamlesh Singh breaks down the Cockroach Janta Party phenomenon and points out that the Gen Z in India is not a monolith, like the ones in Nepal or Lanka.

Delhi roads are burning at 65°C. So, why do weather apps show just 42°C?A ground report from Delhi's Nand Nagri area found that road and vehicle surfaces reach 65°C under direct sunlight even as weather apps show 42°C at the same location.

Don't play with fire: Humayun Kabir warns Bengal govt on Eid prayer, qurbaniThe confrontation between Humayun Kabir and the BJP government in West Bengal snowballed as the Opposition MLA warned it over cow slaughter restrictions, curbs on Eid namaz and mandatory singing of Vande Mataram in madrasas.

Viral 'Donald Trump' buffalo to be slaughtered on Eid-al-Adha in BangladeshA rare albino buffalo nicknamed 'Donald Trump' has become a viral attraction, with crowds flocking to see the animal for its pink-toned skin and golden hair that many say resembles the US President's trademark hairstyle.

Want Green Card? US says leave country first, re-enter with immigrant visaThe Trump administration has said that migrants seeking Green Cards must leave the US and apply from their home countries. The change could affect students, workers and many Indians waiting to shift to permanent residency.

Militants with drone jammers. Why Manipur arms haul is worrisomeSecurity forces seized a large cache of weapons and drone jammers from UNLF(P) in Manipur days ago. The recovery points to militant access to advanced counter-drone systems and cross-border supply routes.

Trump 'seriously considering' fresh strikes on Iran amid faltering truce talksTrump met his senior national security team on Friday to discuss the Iran war as Pakistan Army chief Asim Munir and a Qatari delegation arrived in Tehran for last-ditch talks aimed at securing a deal.

Diminishes us: Gul Panag shreds Dhruv Rathee over 'humiliate PM everywhere' remarkActor Gul Panag, a long-time critic of the Narendra Modi government and a former AAP leader, slammed YouTuber Dhruv Rathee, who urged foreign journalists to "humiliate" the Indian Prime Minister everywhere abroad. She said Rathee's comments diminished "the institution and, ultimately, the Indians".
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump's intelligence chiefTulsi Gabbard has resigned as Director of National Intelligence in President Donald Trump's administration, citing her husband Abraham's diagnosis with a rare form of bone cancer.

All 50 hottest cities in the world today are in India. List hereFriday morning rankings showed all 50 of the world's hottest cities were in India, led by Balangir at 45°C. The list underlined the scale of the 2026 heatwave as the IMD kept severe alerts in place across several states.

Aishwarya Rai Bachchan wears Amit Aggarwal's 1,500-hour couture creation at CannesAishwarya Rai Bachchan has finally arrived at the 79th edition of the Cannes Film Festival in a dramatic blue sculpted gown, serving signature glamour, drama and undeniable red carpet presence.
Watch: Starship explodes in Indian Ocean seconds after water landingSpaceX brought Starship down in a planned splashdown zone in the Indian Ocean, where it exploded on impact.

TCS deducted 50% variable pay of some employees in April salary, here is whyEmployees at TCS reportedly saw lower variable pay in April salaries after the company linked payouts to office attendance and business performance. The latest appraisal cycle has also triggered confusion among some workers over revised salary structures and smaller hikes.

Now, a Pakistan and ISI-asset claim against Cockroach party, its founderCockroach Janta Party has exploded across social media in India ever since it was launched a week ago. With virality, has come a raft of allegations. Several BJP leaders have alleged that the party had 50% followers from Pakistan and its founder was an 'ISI asset'. What's the truth?

sports.headline: IPL Play Of The Day: Heinrich Klaasen makes Hazlegod look human in Uppal carnage SRH vs RCB, IPL Play of the Day: Heinrich Klaasen proved that Josh Hazlewood was indeed human as he destroyed the Australian pacer during the SRH vs RCB clash. Klaasen's takedown of Hazlewood proved to be the difference as Hyderabad enter Eliminator in destructive form.

Virat Kohli ignores handshake with Travis Head, moment goes viral: Watch Virat Kohli sparked controversy as he ignored the customary post-match handshake with Travis Head following the SRH vs RCB game in Hyderabad.

RCB's 255 chase approach against SRH split fans over intent IPL 2026: RCB secured the bigger prize of Qualifier 1 despite a crushing 55-run defeat to Sunrisers Hyderabad. But their surprisingly cautious approach in a 256-run chase has now sparked a major debate among fans online.

Sabalenka cuts short media duties in protest against French Open prize money Aryna Sabalenka cut short her French Open media duties as players staged a 15-minute work-to-rule protest over prize money. The action underlined a wider push for a bigger share of Grand Slam revenue, benefits and influence.

Dream wicket for me: Sakib Hussain relishing Virat Kohli's dismissal IPL 2026: Sakib Hussain dismissed Virat Kohli in Sunrisers Hyderabad's 55-run win over Royal Challengers Bengaluru. The wicket fulfilled a personal dream for the SRH fast-bowler even as the franchise failed to secure a top-two finish.

RCB get Qualifier 1, but SRH arrive at playoffs like absolute villains IPL 2026: RCB may have secured the safer Qualifier 1 route, but it was Sunrisers Hyderabad who walked away looking like the playoffs' biggest danger. Abhishek Sharma, Ishan Kishan and Heinrich Klaasen tore apart the defending champions in a brutal Hyderabad batting show.

Virat Kohli and Travis Head share verbal banter during SRH vs RCB Virat Kohli repeatedly needled Travis Head during RCB's chase at Uppal, even gesturing for the Australian to bowl off-spin. The exchanges sharpened the mood of a high-stakes night shaped by SRH's 256 and Kohli's early dismissal.

Chinnaswamy revamp begins days after Bengaluru loses IPL 2026 final IPL 2026: Days after Bengaluru lost hosting rights for the IPL 2026 final, the iconic M Chinnaswamy Stadium is now heading towards a major makeover. KSCA president Venkatesh Prasad admitted that the venue's limited capacity may have contributed to the BCCI shifting the summit clash to Ahmedabad.

OG T20 league IPL reason behind India's consecutive World Cup wins: Guptill Martin Guptill said the IPL has directly shaped India's back-to-back T20 World Cup wins. He argued the league's scale, talent pipeline and competitive edge keep it ahead of every rival T20 league in the world.

ISL clubs warn AIFF that uncertainty could impact long-term league commitment Several ISL clubs have warned the AIFF they may scale back their commitment beyond the current season. The statement sharpens pressure ahead of the Special General Body Meeting on the league's commercial future.

Indian football board set for crucial meeting on ISL commercial rights battle The AIFF will hold a Special General Body Meeting on Saturday to discuss the ISL's commercial future and proposed constitutional changes. The meeting comes amid opposition from clubs over rights structure and objections over the General Body's composition.

Animesh Kujur breaks national 100m record to seal Commonwealth games qualificationAnimesh Kujur reclaimed the 100m national record minutes after Gurindervir Singh took it at the Federation Cup. His 10.15 seconds sealed Commonwealth Games qualification and sharpened India's emerging sprint rivalry.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi sparks Bihar IPL dream as CM makes big claimBihar Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary on Friday backed the idea of having an Indian Premier League (IPL) team from the state, saying the government is working in "mission mode" to improve cricket infrastructure and create opportunities for young players.

KKR suffer big blow as Angkrish Raghuvanshi ruled out of must-win DC clashKolkata Knight Riders have lost Angkrish Raghuvanshi to a concussion and a fractured finger ahead of the Delhi Capitals game. The setback weakens both their batting and wicketkeeping options with playoff qualification still on the line.

Ricky Ponting distances himself from PBKS off-field social media dramaRicky Ponting said Punjab Kings have not been distracted by the latest social media controversy involving Arshdeep Singh and Harpreet Brar. The remarks come with Punjab's season slipping badly and their playoff hopes hanging by a thread.

Novak Djokovic breaks silence on French Open media boycott over pay rowNovak Djokovic has opened up on his support for the growing player protest over prize money and revenue sharing at the French Open, insisting that tennis players need to stand "more united" in their fight for fairer treatment from Grand Slam organisers.

Shan Masood wants to continue as Pakistan Test captain but under one conditionShan Masood has told the PCB he wants to continue as Pakistan's Test captain. But he wants a steadier selection process and team management after repeated changes.

Proper ISL next season assured: Another promise by AIFF chief Kalyan ChaubeyAfter East Bengal's title win, Kalyan Chaubey said the ISL will return in full next season. His assurance comes after a delayed, shortened campaign left the league's future under scrutiny.

Vijay Shankar retires from IPL and domestic cricket, to prolong career overseasVijay Shankar has retired from the IPL and domestic cricket to seek more opportunities overseas. The move follows after being unsold during the IPL 2026 auction despite registering as an uncapped player.

Sourav Ganguly's viral tufani gully cricket heroics remind fans of vintage DadaFormer Indian captain Sourav Ganguly turned back the clock in a viral gully cricket video, reminding fans of his vintage brilliance at the age of 53. Ganguly hammered 132 runs off just 68 deliveries.

AFC relegates Indian clubs to third-tier Challenge League play-offsThe AFC has cut India's 2027-28 club slot to a single AFC Challenge League play-off place. The move follows a shortened ISL season, weaker rankings and wider licensing concerns.

Former Spain and Chelsea defender Cesar Azpilicueta announces retirementCesar Azpilicueta has announced he will retire at the end of the season after a career across Spain, France and England. He leaves as one of Chelsea's modern greats, his standing built on consistency, leadership and silverware.

Liverpool boss Arne Slot breaks silence on Mohamed Salah's social media rantLiverpool manager Arne Slot has responded for the first time to Mohamed Salah's controversial social media post, saying Liverpool's focus remains on securing Champions League qualification despite tensions between the two.

Tamil Nadu CM Vijay gifted Virat Kohli signed MRF batTamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay was pictured with a Virat Kohli-signed MRF bat after meeting company representatives. The viral image fused cricket fandom with industrial lobbying around MRF's proposed ₹5,300-crore tyre plant.

Jam with Jemimah 2.0: Gavaskar joins India star for duet before T20 World CupSunil Gavaskar and Jemimah Rodrigues have reunited in a new Instagram reel, as they engaged in a new jamming session ahead of the T20 World Cup 2026.

Alvaro Arbeloa leaves Real Madrid: Will not work with Jose MourinhoReal Madrid head coach Alvaro Arbeloa is leaving the club at the end of the season. The outgoing manager has also confirmed that he has no interest in working with Mourinho if he returns to coach the club this year.

Don't ask me why I am leaving: Guardiola confirms his Manchester City departureManchester City manager Pep Guardiola will leave the club at the end of the season, ending a remarkable 10-year spell that changed the course of the club's history and redefined standards in English football.

Manchester United confirm Michael Carrick as new full-time head coachManchester United have confirmed Michael Carrick as their new full-time head coach after his strong spell as caretaker. The decision follows a late-season surge that restored Champions League football and some order to a chaotic campaign.

England's FIFA World Cup squad shocker: Foden, Palmer and Trent axed by TuchelThomas Tuchel has announced his England squad for this summer's FIFA World Cup, with several big-name exclusions including Phil Foden, Cole Palmer and Morgan Gibbs-White.

SRH playoff scenario: Can Pat Cummins' team finish in top 2?3rd-placed SunRisers Hyderabad will play their final league stage game of IPL 2026 on Friday. CSK's heavy defeat to Gujarat Titans on Thursday has made a top-two finish highly unlikely for SRH, though a mathematical possibility still remains.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
